summary: Sophia brought in her right Signia Styletto X as it had stopped charging. Had a look at the charging port and found some moisture damage and a bit of debris built up inside. Cleaned it out and tested it and it is charging normally again. Talked to her about looking after the aids, particularly using a drying kit overnight and keeping them away from moisture. Gave the device back to her in working order. Reminded her of her next scheduled appointment.

Patient referred by GP, reports difficulty hearing family members and in noisy environments. Audiogram shows mild to moderate high-frequency hearing loss in both ears. Patient informed about results and is interested in hearing aids. Demo appointment booked for next week.

Fitted bilateral ReSound OMNIA 9 demo aids and set the gain to 80% in first-time user mode. Patient noticed clearer speech and was informed about the adjustment period. Patient will take the aids home and return for follow-up.

Fitted bilateral ReSound OMNIA 5 aids and programmed them to his audiogram. Ran real-ear measurements and output was within a good range. Provided instructions on use, charging, volume adjustment, and app setup. Scheduled follow-up in four weeks.

David came in for a fitting for his right ear only. Fitted a ReSound OMNIA 9 RIC to the right ear and programmed it to his current audiogram. He has worn aids before and settled quickly. Discussed differences between his old device and OMNIA 9, focusing on Bluetooth streaming and updated noise management. He was happy with the sound and had no concerns. Booked for a review in six weeks.
